{\n1232\t\t\tc.SecurityContext = securityContext\n1233\t\t}\n1234\t}\n1235\t\n1236\t\n1237\t// =============================================================================\n1238\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n1239\t// =============================================================================\n1240\t\n1241\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n1242\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n1243\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n1244\t\tif err != nil {\n1245\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n1246\t\t}\n1247\t\n1248\t\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n1249\t\tif err != nil {\n1250\t\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n1251\t\t\tdesc = \"\"\n1252\t\t}\n1253\t\n1254\t\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n1255\t}\n1256\t\n1257\t// =============================================================================\n1258\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n1259\t// =============================================================================\n1260\t\n1261\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n1262\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\n1263\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\n1264\t\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n1265\t\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n1266\t}\n1267\t\n1268\t// global tracker instance\n1269\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n1270\t\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n1271\t}\n1272\t\n1273\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n1274\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n1275\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n1276\t//\n1277\t// Parameters:\n1278\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n1279\t//\n1280\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\n1281\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n1282\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n1283\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n1284\t\n1285\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n1286\t\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n1287\t}\n1288\t\n1289\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n1290\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n1291\t// configuration issues.\n1292\t//\n1293\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\n1294\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n1295\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n1296\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n1297\t\n1298\t\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n1299\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n1300\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n1301\t\t}\n1302\t\treturn result\n1303\t}\n1304\t\n1305\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n1306\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\n1307\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n1308\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n1309\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n1310\t\n1311\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n1312\t}\n1313\t\n1314\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n1315\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\n1316\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n1317\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n1318\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n1319\t\n1320\t\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n1321\t}\n1322\t\n1323\t// =============================================================================\n1324\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n1325\t// =============================================================================\n1326\t\n1327\t// FormatError creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum.\n1328\t// This is the primary error formatting function that provides type-safe error classification.\n1329\t//\n1330\t// This function uses ErrorType enum for consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n1331\t// For backward compatibility with string-based error types, use FormatErrorString.\n1332\t//\n1333\t// ErrorType to Message Mapping:\n1334\t// The ErrorType enum provides type-safe error classification with the following mappings:\n1335\t//   - ErrTypeRequired (required): Required field is missing or empty\n1336\t//   - ErrTypeFormat (format): Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n1337\t//   - ErrTypeRange (range): Value is outside acceptable numeric range (min/max)\n1338\t//   - ErrTypeLength (length): String length or collection size is invalid\n1339\t//   - ErrTypeType (type): Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n1340\t//   - ErrTypeValue (value): Value is invalid for domain-specific reasons\n1341\t//   - ErrTypeDuplicate (duplicate): Duplicate value detected\n1342\t//   - ErrTypeConflict (conflict): Conflict with existing values or constraints\n1343\t//   - ErrTypeUnknown (unknown): Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n1344\t//\n1345\t// Parameters:\n1346\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1347\t//   - message: The error message content\n1348\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1349\t//\n1350\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure: \"[errorType] fieldName: message\"\n1351\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1352\t//\n1353\t// Example usage:\n1354\t//\n1355\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1356\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n1357\t//\n1358\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1359\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n1360\t//\n1361\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n1362\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n1363\t//\n1364\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"\", \"email\")\n1365\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: email validation failed\" (empty message fallback)\n1366\tfunc FormatError(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1367\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n1368\t\t// This converts the typed ErrorType enum to its string representation\n1369\t\t// for consistent message formatting across the codebase\n1370\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1371\t\n1372\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n1373\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1374\t\n1375\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1376\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1377\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n1378\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1379\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1380\t\t\t} else {\n1381\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1382\t\t\t}\n1383\t\t}\n1384\t\n1385\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n1386\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n1387\t}\n1388\t\n1389\t// FormatErrorString creates a formatted error message using string error type.\n1390\t// This function provides backward compatibility for code that uses string-based error types.\n1391\t//\n1392\t// This function validates string error types against the ErrorType enum for type-safe\n1393\t// error classification while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value.\n1394\t//\n1395\t// Error Type Validation:\n1396\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n1397\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n1398\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n1399\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1400\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n1401\t//\n1402\t// Parameters:\n1403\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error as a string (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n1404\t//   - message: The error message content\n1405\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (variadic parameter)\n1406\t//\n1407\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n1408\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1409\t//\n1410\t// Example usage:\n1411\t//\n1412\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n1413\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1414\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n1415\t//\n1416\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\")\n1417\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n1418\t//\n1419\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n1420\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorString(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n1421\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n1422\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n1423\t//\n1424\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n1425\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1426\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n1427\tfunc FormatErrorString(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n1428\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args first\n1429\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n1430\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n1431\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n1432\t\t}\n1433\t\n1434\t\t// Check if errorType is whitespace-only (not truly empty)\n1435\t\t// Whitespace-only error types should be tracked as invalid\n1436\t\t// because they are likely user errors, not intentional defaults\n1437\t\toriginalHasContent := errorType != \"\"\n1438\t\ttrimmedType := strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n1439\t\tisWhitespaceOnly := originalHasContent && trimmedType == \"\"\n1440\t\n1441\t\t// Track whitespace-only error types as invalid\n1442\t\tif isWhitespaceOnly {\n1443\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n1444\t\t}\n1445\t\n1446\t\t// Trim whitespace from errorType before validation\n1447\t\terrorType = trimmedType\n1448\t\n1449\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n1450\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n1451\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n1452\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n1453\t\n1454\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n1455\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the trimmed errorType wasn't \"unknown\" (case-insensitive),\n1456\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n1457\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && !strings.EqualFold(errorType, \"unknown\") {\n1458\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n1459\t\t}\n1460\t\n1461\t\t// Trim whitespace from message and fieldName before checking\n1462\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1463\t\tfieldNameStr = strings.TrimSpace(fieldNameStr)\n1464\t\n1465\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1466\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1467\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided (after trimming)\n1468\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n1469\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n1470\t\t\t} else {\n1471\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1472\t\t\t}\n1473\t\t}\n1474\t\n1475\t\t// Handle empty errorType (after trimming) - use fallback\n1476\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n1477\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n1478\t\t}\n1479\t\n1480\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the processed error type string\n1481\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n1482\t}\n1483\t\n1484\t// FormatErrorWithType is an alias for FormatError for backward compatibility.\n1485\t// This function calls FormatError with the same parameters.\n1486\t//\n1487\t// Deprecated: Use FormatError directly with ErrorType enum parameter instead.\n1488\t// This function is maintained for backward compatibility with existing code.\n1489\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1490\t\treturn FormatError(errorType, message, fieldName)\n1491\t}\n1492\t\n1493\t// =============================================================================\n1494\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n1495\t// =============================================================================\n1496\t\n1497\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\n1498\ttype QuoteStyle string\n1499\t\n1500\tconst (\n1501\t\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n1502\t\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n1503\t\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n1504\t\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n1505\t\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n1506\t\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n1507\t\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n1508\t\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n1509\t)\n1510\t\n1511\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\n1512\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\n1513\t\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n1514\t\tprefix     string\n1515\t}\n1516\t\n1517\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\n1518\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n1519\t\n1520\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n1521\t// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n1522\t//\n1523\t// Example usage:\n1524\t//\n1525\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1526\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n1527\t//\n1528\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n1529\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n1530\tfunc WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n1531\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n1532\t\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n1533\t\t}\n1534\t}\n1535\t\n1536\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n1537\t// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n1538\t// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n1539\t//\n1540\t// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n1541\t//\n1542\t// Example usage:\n1543\t//\n1544\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n1545\t//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n1546\t//\n1547\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"request\"))\n1548\t//\t// Returns: request.email\n1549\t//\n1550\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"data\"), WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1551\t//\t// Returns: data.\"users\"[0].\"email\"\n1552\tfunc WithPrefix(prefix string) FieldRefOption {\n1553\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n1554\t\t\tc.prefix = prefix\n1555\t\t}\n1556\t}\n1557\t\n1558\t// =============================================================================\n1559\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n1560\t// =============================================================================\n1561\t\n1562\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n1563\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n1564\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n1565\t//\n1566\t// Parameters:\n1567\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n1568\t//\n1569\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n1570\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n1571\t//\n1572\t// Example usage:\n1573\t//\n1574\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n1575\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n1576\t//\n1577\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n1578\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n1579\t//\n1580\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n1581\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n1582\t//\n1583\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n1584\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n1585\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n1586\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n1587\t\n1588\t\t// Handle empty path\n1589\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n1590\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1591\t\t}\n1592\t\n1593\t\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n1594\t\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n1595\t}\n1596\t\n1597\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n1598\t// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n1599\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n1600\t\tif path == \"\" {\n1601\t\t\treturn \"\"\n1602\t\t}\n1603\t\n1604\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n1605\t\tvar result []string\n1606\t\n1607\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n1608\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n1609\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n1610\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1611\t\t\t}\n1612\t\n1613\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n1614\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n1615\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n1616\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n1617\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n1618\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n1619\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n1620\t\t\t\t} else {\n1621\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n1622\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1623\t\t\t\t}\n1624\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n1625\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n1626\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1627\t\t\t} else {\n1628\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n1629\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n1630\t\t\t}\n1631\t\t}\n1632\t\n1633\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n1634\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1635\t\t}\n1636\t\n1637\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n1638\t}\n1639\t\n1640\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n1641\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n1642\t\tif s == \"\" {\n1643\t\t\treturn false\n1644\t\t}\n1645\t\n1646\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n1647\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n1648\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n1649\t\t\t\treturn false\n1650\t\t\t}\n1651\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n1652\t\t}\n1653\t\n1654\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n1655\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n1656\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n1657\t\t\t\treturn false\n1658\t\t\t}\n1659\t\t}\n1660\t\n1661\t\treturn true\n1662\t}\n1663\t\n1664\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n1665\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n1666\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n1667\t}\n1668\t\n1669\t// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n1670\t// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n1671\t// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n1672\t// for field names through variadic options.\n1673\t//\n1674\t// Parameters:\n1675\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n1676\t//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n1677\t//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1678\t//\n1679\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n1680\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n1681\t//\n1682\t// Example usage:\n1683\t//\n1684\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n1685\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n1686\t//\n1687\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n1688\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n1689\t//\n1690\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"request\")\n1691\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n1692\t//\n1693\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n1694\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n1695\t//\n1696\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n1697\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n1698\t//\n1699\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n1700\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n1701\t//\n1702\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"data.items\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(Backtick))\n1703\t//\t// Returns: `data`.`items`\n1704\t//\n1705\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"\")\n1706\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n1707\t//\n1708\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n1709\t//\t// Returns: \"request\"\n1710\tfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n1711\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n1712\t\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n1713\t\n1714\t\t// Apply options\n1715\t\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n1716\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1717\t\t\topt(config)\n1718\t\t}\n1719\t\n1720\t\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n1721\t\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n1722\t\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n1723\t\t}\n1724\t\n1725\t\t// Handle empty field path\n1726\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n1727\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1728\t\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is empty\n1729\t\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1730\t\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix != \"(unknown field)\" {\n1731\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPrefix\n1732\t\t\t\t}\n1733\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1734\t\t\t}\n1735\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1736\t\t}\n1737\t\n1738\t\t// Normalize field path to handle array indices\n1739\t\tnormalizedPath := FormatFieldPath(fieldPath)\n1740\t\n1741\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n1742\t\tif normalizedPath == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1743\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1744\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n1745\t\t\t}\n1746\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n1747\t\t}\n1748\t\n1749\t\t// Apply quote style if specified\n1750\t\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n1751\t\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, config.quoteStyle)\n1752\t\t}\n1753\t\n1754\t\t// Add prefix if provided\n1755\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n1756\t\t\t// Normalize prefix as well in case it contains array indices\n1757\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n1758\t\n1759\t\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n1760\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n1761\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1762\t\t\t}\n1763\t\n1764\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n1765\t\t}\n1766\t\n1767\t\treturn normalizedPath\n1768\t}\n1769\t\n1770\t// applyQuoteStyle applies quotes to field names in a normalized path.\n1771\t// Array indices are NOT quoted (e.g., \"users[0].email\" becomes \"'users'[0].'email'\").\n1772\tfunc applyQuoteStyle(path string, style QuoteStyle) string {\n1773\t\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n1774\t\t\treturn path\n1775\t\t}\n1776\t\n1777\t\tvar result strings.Builder\n1778\t\tvar fieldName strings.Builder\n1779\t\tinBrackets := false\n1780\t\n1781\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(path); i++ {\n1782\t\t\tr := rune(path[i])\n1783\t\n1784\t\t\tswitch {\n1785\t\t\tcase r == '.':\n1786\t\t\t\t// End of field name, add quoted version\n1787\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1788\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1789\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1790\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1791\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1792\t\t\t\t}\n1793\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('.')\n1794\t\n1795\t\t\tcase r == '[':\n1796\t\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n1797\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1798\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1799\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1800\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1801\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n1802\t\t\t\t}\n1803\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('[')\n1804\t\t\t\tinBrackets = true\n1805\t\n1806\t\t\tcase r == ']':\n1807\t\t\t\t// End of array index, don't quote\n1808\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(']')\n1809\t\t\t\tinBrackets = false\n1810\t\n1811\t\t\tdefault:\n1812\t\t\t\t// Only accumulate field name characters when NOT inside brackets\n1813\t\t\t\tif !inBrackets {\n1814\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.WriteRune(r)\n1815\t\t\t\t} else {\n1816\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(r)\n1817\t\t\t\t}\n1818\t\t\t}\n1819\t\t}\n1820\t\n1821\t\t// Don't forget the last field name\n1822\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n1823\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1824\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n1825\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n1826\t\t}\n1827\t\n1828\t\treturn result.String()\n1829\t}\n1830\t\n1831\t// =============================================================================\n1832\t// CATEGORY-AWARE FORMATTING WITH SEVERITY\n1833\t// =============================================================================\n1834\t\n1835\t// FormatErrorWithSeverity creates a formatted error message using ErrorType enum\n1836\t// with explicit severity support. This function provides type-safe error classification\n1837\t// with severity-based formatting.\n1838\t//\n1839\t// This function respects error severity levels and applies appropriate formatting:\n1840\t// - Critical errors: Bold styling with prominent indicators\n1841\t// - High errors: Warning indicators with emphasis\n1842\t// - Medium errors: Standard formatting with indicators\n1843\t// - Low errors: Subtle formatting\n1844\t// - Info errors: Minimal formatting\n1845\t//\n1846\t// Parameters:\n1847\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n1848\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity level (e.g., SeverityCritical, SeverityHigh)\n1849\t//   - message: The error message content\n1850\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n1851\t//\n1852\t// Returns a formatted error message string with severity-based styling.\n1853\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n1854\t//\n1855\t// Example usage:\n1856\t//\n1857\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeRequired, validate.SeverityHigh, \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n1858\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] HIGH [required] email: This field is required\"\n1859\t//\n1860\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithSeverity(validate.ErrTypeFormat, validate.SeverityMedium, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n1861\t//\t// Returns: \"[■] MED [format] Invalid email format\"\n1862\tfunc FormatErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, severity ErrorSeverity, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1863\t\t// Get default severity if not provided\n1864\t\tif severity == \"\" {\n1865\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1866\t\t}\n1867\t\n1868\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1869\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n1870\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1871\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1872\t\n1873\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n1874\t\tif message == \"\" {\n1875\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1876\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n1877\t\t\t} else {\n1878\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n1879\t\t\t}\n1880\t\t}\n1881\t\n1882\t\t// Build severity prefix\n1883\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n1884\t\n1885\t\t// Build the formatted message\n1886\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1887\t\n1888\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n1889\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n1890\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n1891\t\n1892\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n1893\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorTypeStr))\n1894\t\n1895\t\t// Add field name if present\n1896\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n1897\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n1898\t\t}\n1899\t\n1900\t\t// Add message\n1901\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n1902\t\n1903\t\treturn builder.String()\n1904\t}\n1905\t\n1906\t// formatSeverityPrefix creates a severity prefix with indicator and tag.\n1907\tfunc formatSeverityPrefix(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1908\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n1909\t\ttag := severityTag(severity)\n1910\t\n1911\t\tif indicator != \"\" && tag != \"\" {\n1912\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s %s]\", indicator, tag)\n1913\t\t} else if indicator != \"\" {\n1914\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", indicator)\n1915\t\t} else if tag != \"\" {\n1916\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", tag)\n1917\t\t}\n1918\t\n1919\t\treturn \"\"\n1920\t}\n1921\t\n1922\t// severityTag returns a short tag prefix for severity levels.\n1923\tfunc severityTag(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1924\t\tswitch severity {\n1925\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n1926\t\t\treturn \"CRIT\"\n1927\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n1928\t\t\treturn \"HIGH\"\n1929\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n1930\t\t\treturn \"MED\"\n1931\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n1932\t\t\treturn \"LOW\"\n1933\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n1934\t\t\treturn \"INFO\"\n1935\t\tdefault:\n1936\t\t\treturn \"UNK\"\n1937\t\t}\n1938\t}\n1939\t\n1940\t// FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity creates a formatted error message with\n1941\t// both category and severity awareness. This is the most comprehensive formatting\n1942\t// function that respects both error categorization and severity levels.\n1943\t//\n1944\t// This function applies category-specific formatting:\n1945\t// - HTTP errors: Technical focus with status code details\n1946\t// - Content errors: Response structure and body focus\n1947\t// - Validation errors: Field-level validation focus\n1948\t// - Performance errors: Timing and rate limit focus\n1949\t// - Security errors: Authentication and authorization focus\n1950\t//\n1951\t// Combined with severity-based styling for maximum clarity.\n1952\t//\n1953\t// Parameters:\n1954\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n1955\t//   - message: The error message content\n1956\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n1957\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions for customization\n1958\t//\n1959\t// Returns a formatted error message with category and severity awareness.\n1960\t//\n1961\t// Example usage:\n1962\t//\n1963\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1964\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1965\t//\t    \"Field is required\",\n1966\t//\t    \"email\",\n1967\t//\t    validate.WithSeverityOverride(validate.SeverityCritical),\n1968\t//\t    validate.WithCategoryHint(validate.CategoryValidation),\n1969\t//\t)\n1970\t//\t// Returns: \"[🚨 CRIT] [Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\n1971\tfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1972\t\terrorType ErrorType,\n1973\t\tmessage string,\n1974\t\tfieldName string,\n1975\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n1976\t) string {\n1977\t\t// Get default category and severity\n1978\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1979\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1980\t\n1981\t\t// Apply options to override defaults\n1982\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n1983\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1984\t\t\topt(config)\n1985\t\t}\n1986\t\n1987\t\t// Apply overrides if specified\n1988\t\tif config.SeverityOverride != \"\" {\n1989\t\t\tseverity = config.SeverityOverride\n1990\t\t}\n1991\t\tif config.CategoryHint != \"\" {\n1992\t\t\tcategory = config.CategoryHint\n1993\t\t}\n1994\t\n1995\t\t// Trim whitespace from inputs\n1996\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n1997\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n1998\t\n1999\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n2000\t\tif message == \"\" {\n2001\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n2002\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n2003\t\t\t} else {\n2004\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n2005\t\t\t}\n2006\t\t}\n2007\t\n2008\t\t// Build severity prefix\n2009\t\tseverityPrefix := formatSeverityPrefix(severity)\n2010\t\n2011\t\t// Build category-specific formatting\n2012\t\tcategoryPrefix := formatCategoryPrefix(category)\n2013\t\n2014\t\t// Build the formatted message\n2015\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n2016\t\n2017\t\t// Add severity indicator and tag\n2018\t\tbuilder.WriteString(severityPrefix)\n2019\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n2020\t\n2021\t\t// Add category prefix if category is not custom\n2022\t\tif category != CategoryCustom && category != \"\" {\n2023\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(categoryPrefix)\n2024\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" \")\n2025\t\t}\n2026\t\n2027\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n2028\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType.String()))\n2029\t\n2030\t\t// Add field name if present\n2031\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n2032\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n2033\t\t}\n2034\t\n2035\t\t// Add message\n2036\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n2037\t\n2038\t\t// Add category-specific suffix if applicable\n2039\t\tbuilder.WriteString(formatCategorySuffix(category, config))\n2040\t\n2041\t\treturn builder.String()\n2042\t}\n2043\t\n2044\t// formatCategoryPrefix creates a category-specific prefix.\n2045\tfunc formatCategoryPrefix(category ErrorCategory) string {\n2046\t\tswitch category {\n2047\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n2048\t\t\treturn \"[HTTP]\"\n2049\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n2050\t\t\treturn \"[Content]\"\n2051\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n2052\t\t\treturn \"[Validation]\"\n2053\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n2054\t\t\treturn \"[Performance]\"\n2055\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n2056\t\t\treturn \"[Security]\"\n2057\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n2058\t\t\treturn \"[Custom]\"\n2059\t\tdefault:\n2060\t\t\treturn \"\"\n2061\t\t}\n2062\t}\n2063\t\n2064\t// formatCategorySuffix creates a category-specific suffix with additional context.\n2065\tfunc formatCategorySuffix(category ErrorCategory, config *FormatConfig) string {\n2066\t\t// Handle nil config gracefully\n2067\t\tif config == nil {\n2068\t\t\treturn \"\"\n2069\t\t}\n2070\t\n2071\t\tvar suffix strings.Builder\n2072\t\n2073\t\tswitch category {\n2074\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n2075\t\t\t// Add HTTP-specific context\n2076\t\t\tif config.StatusCode != 0 {\n2077\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (status: %d)\", config.StatusCode))\n2078\t\t\t}\n2079\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n2080\t\t\t// Add performance-specific context\n2081\t\t\tif config.Timeout > 0 {\n2082\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (timeout: %dms)\", config.Timeout))\n2083\t\t\t}\n2084\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n2085\t\t\t// Add security-specific context\n2086\t\t\tif config.SecurityContext != \"\" {\n2087\t\t\t\tsuffix.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (security: %s)\", config.SecurityContext))\n2088\t\t\t}\n2089\t\t}\n2090\t\n2091\t\treturn suffix.String()\n2092\t}\n2093\t\n2094\t// =============================================================================\n2095\t// STREAMLINED VALIDATION ERROR FORMATTING HELPER\n2096\t// =============================================================================\n2097\t\n2098\t// FormatValidationErrorHelper is a streamlined helper function for formatting validation errors consistently.\n2099\t// This function provides a simple, unified interface for creating validation errors with optional\n2100\t// context, expected/actual values, and custom suggestions.\n2101\t//\n2102\t// This helper is designed to be the primary entry point for validation error formatting,\n2103\t// offering a balance between simplicity and flexibility. It handles the most common validation\n2104\t// error scenarios with a clean, easy-to-use API.\n2105\t//\n2106\t// # Parameters\n2107\t//\n2108\t//   - errorType: The category of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n2109\t//     Required. This identifies what kind of validation was performed.\n2110\t//\n2111\t//   - expected: The expected value (can be any type: int, string, []int, etc.)\n2112\t//     Optional. Pass nil if not applicable for your validation type.\n2113\t//\n2114\t//   - actual: The actual value received (can be any type)\n2115\t//     Optional. Pass nil if not applicable for your validation type.\n2116\t//\n2117\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions for customization (variadic parameter)\n2118\t//     Use validation helper options like WithContext(), WithSuggestions(), etc.\n2119\t//\n2120\t// # Returns\n2121\t//\n2122\t// A ValidationError struct populated with all provided fields that implements\n2123\t// the error interface and can be used immediately in error handling, logging, or test assertions.\n2124\t//\n2125\t// # Features\n2126\t//\n2127\t//   - **Unified Interface**: Single function for all validation error types\n2128\t//   - **Type Safety**: Works with any value type for expected/actual\n2129\t//   - **Auto-generated Suggestions**: Smart suggestions based on error type and values\n2130\t//   - **Custom Suggestions**: Override with domain-specific suggestions\n2131\t//   - **Flexible Context**: Add contextual information for debugging\n2132\t//   - **Optional Fields**: Only provide what you need for your scenario\n2133\t//\n2134\t// # Common Usage Patterns\n2135\t//\n2136\t// Basic usage with just error type and values:\n2137\t//\n2138\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"status_code\", 200, 404)\n2139\t//\n2140\t// With context for debugging:\n2141\t//\n2142\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"status_code\", 200, 404,\n2143\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\"))\n2144\t//\n2145\t// With custom suggestions:\n2146\t//\n2147\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"custom_field\", \"required\", \"\",\n2148\t//\t    validate.WithFieldName(\"email\"),\n2149\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Email address is required\", \"Please provide a valid email\"))\n2150\t//\n2151\t// Complete example with all options:\n2152\t//\n2153\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"error_message\",\n2154\t//\t    \"User .* not found\",\n2155\t//\t    \"Internal server error\",\n2156\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"User profile lookup API\"),\n2157\t//\t    validate.WithFieldName(\"error\"),\n2158\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check if user exists\", \"Verify user ID\"))\n2159\t//\n2160\t// # Supported Error Types\n2161\t//\n2162\t// Common error types (auto-recognized for suggestions):\n2163\t//   - \"status_code\": HTTP status code validation\n2164\t//   - \"error_message\": Error message pattern matching\n2165\t//   - \"content_type\": Content-Type header validation\n2166\t//   - \"status_code_range\": Status code range validation (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n2167\t//   - \"cors_headers\": CORS header validation\n2168\t//   - \"response_structure\": Response body structure validation\n2169\t//   - Custom types: Any string works for domain-specific validation\n2170\t//\n2171\t// # Example Output\n2172\t//\n2173\t//\terr := validate.FormatValidationErrorHelper(\"status_code\", 200, 404,\n2174\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\"))\n2175\t//\n2176\t//\t// Error() output:\n2177\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n2178\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n2179\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n2180\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n2181\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n2182\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n2183\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n2184\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n2185\t//\n2186\t// # Error Interface Implementation\n2187\t//\n2188\t// The returned ValidationError implements the error interface, so you can:\n2189\t//\n2190\t//\t// Return directly as error\n2191\t//\tif err != nil {\n2192\t//\t    return err\n2193\t//\t}\n2194\t//\n2195\t//\t// Use in logging\n2196\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n2197\t//\n2198\t//\t// Use in test assertions\n2199\t//\tassert.Equal(t, \"expected message\", err.Error())\n2200\t//\n2201\t// # Auto-generated Suggestions\n2202\t//\n2203\t// If you don't provide custom suggestions, the helper automatically generates them\n2204\t// based on the error type and values:\n2205\t//   - \"status_code\" with 404: Resource and endpoint verification suggestions\n2206\t//   - \"error_message\" with pattern mismatch: Pattern debugging suggestions\n2207\t//   - \"content_type\": Header and format verification suggestions\n2208\t//   - \"status_code_range\": Range-specific debugging suggestions\n2209\t//   - Custom types: Generic validation troubleshooting suggestions\n2210\t//\n2211\t// # When to Use This Helper\n2212\t//\n2213\t// Use FormatValidationErrorHelper when you need:\n2214\t//   - A simple, clean API for validation errors\n2215\t//   - Consistent error formatting across your codebase\n2216\t//   - Type-safe error creation with any value types\n2217\t//   - Optional context and suggestions without complexity\n2218\t//   - A unified entry point for all validation error scenarios\n2219\t//\n2220\t// For advanced scenarios requiring full control over all fields, use\n2221\t// NewValidationFormatter and the builder pattern instead.\n2222\tfunc FormatValidationErrorHelper(errorType string, expected, actual interface{}, options ...FormatOption) ValidationError {\n2223\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n2224\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n2225\t\t\topt(config)\n2226\t\t}\n2227\t\n2228\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(errorType).\n2229\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n2230\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n2231\t\n2232\t\t// Apply optional configuration\n2233\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n2234\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n2235\t\t}\n2236\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n2237\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n2238\t\t}\n2239\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n2240\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n2241\t\t}\n2242\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n2243\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n2244\t\t}\n2245\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n2246\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n2247\t\t}\n2248\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n2249\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n2250\t\t}\n2251\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n2252\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n2253\t\t}\n2254\t\n2255\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n2256\t}\n2257\t\n2258\t// =============================================================================\n2259\t// VALIDATION ERROR HELPER OPTIONS\n2260\t// =============================================================================\n2297\t\n2298\t// formatBasicCategoryPrefix creates a basic category-specific prefix without styling indicators.\n2299\t// \"\"\n2336\t\t}\n2337\t}\n2338\t\n2339\t// =============================================================================\n2340\t// CATEGORY-SPECIFIC FORMATTING FUNCTIONS\n2341\t// =============================================================================\n2342\t\n2343\t// FormatHTTPError creates a formatted error message specifically for HTTP-related errors.\n2344\t// This GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(errorType)\n2360\t\n2361\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n2362\t\tbuilder.WriteString(formatSeverityPrefix(severity))\n2363\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" [HTTP] \")\n2364\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n2365\t\n2366\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n2367\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n2368\t\t}\n2369\t\n2370\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n2371\t\n2372\t\tif statusCode != 0 {\n2373\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (status: %d)\", statusCode))\n2374\t\t}\n2375\t\n2376\t\treturn builder.String()\n2377\t}\n2378\t\n2379\t// FormatValidationErrorWithSeverity creates a formatted error message specifically for validation [range] age: Value out of range (expected: 0-120, actual: 150)\"\n2398\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}) string {\n2399\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n2400\t\n2401\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n2402\t\tbuilder.WriteString(formatSeverityPrefix(severity))\n2403\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" [Validation] \")\n2404\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType.String()))\n2405\t\n2406\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n2407\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n2408\t\t}\n2409\t\n2410\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n2411\t\n2412\t\tif expected != nil || actual != nil {\n2413\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n2414\t\t\tif expected != nil {\n2415\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", expected))\n2416\t\t\t}\n2417\t\t\tif expected != nil && actual != nil {\n2418\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n2419\t\t\t}\n2420\t\t\tif actual != nil {\n2421\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", actual))\n2422\t\t\t}\n2423\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n2424\t\t}\n2425\t\n2426\t\treturn builder.String()\n2427\t}\n2428\t\n2429\t// FormatPerformanceError creates a formatted error message specifically for performance errors.\n2430\t// This function applies performance-specific formatting with timing details.\n2431\t//\n2432\t// Parameters:\n2433\t//   - errorType: The performance error type (e.g., \"timeout\", \"rate_limit\")\n2434\t//   - timeoutMs: The timeout in milliseconds (use 0 if not applicable)\n2435\t//   - message: The error message\n2436\t//   - fieldName: Optional field validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"sync\"\n)\n\n// ValidationFormatter provides a simplified, fluent API for formatting validation errors consistently.\n//\n// This builder wraps the ValidationError functionality with an ergonomic interface that allows\n// you to construct validation errors step-by-step using method chaining.\n//\n// # Design Pattern\n//\n// ValidationFormatter uses the builder pattern to provide:\n//   - Fluent, chainable methods for readable code\n//   - Auto-generated suggestions when not manually specified\n//   - Type-safe error construction\n//   - Consistent error format across all validation types\n//\n// # Required Fields\n//\n// Only validationType is required (via NewValidationFormatter). All other fields are optional.\n// If suggestions are not provided via WithSuggestions, they will be auto-generated based on\n// the validation type and expected/actual values.\n//\n// # Common Usage Pattern\n//\n// The typical workflow is:\n//  1. Create formatter with NewValidationFormatter(type)\n//  2. Chain With* methods to set optional fields\n//  3. Call Format() to generate the ValidationError\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Basic usage with auto-generated suggestions\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n//\t    WithExpected(200).\n//\t    WithActual(404).\n//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n//\t    Format()\n//\n//\t// With custom suggestions\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field\").\n//\t    WithExpected(\"required\").\n//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n//\t    WithFieldName(\"email\").\n//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Email is required\", \"Check user input\").\n//\t    Format()\n//\n// # Extending for New Validation Types\n//\n// To add support for a new validation type:\n//  1. Use NewValidationFormatter(\"your_type\") with a descriptive type name\n//  2. Set appropriate fields using With* methods\n//  3. Optionally provide custom suggestions via WithSuggestions\n//  4. The formatter will automatically include the type in error output\n//\n// # Fields\n//\n// All fields are private to ensure the builder pattern is used correctly.\n// Access them through the public With* methods and Format().\ntype ValidationFormatter struct {\n\tvalidationType string\n\texpected       interface{}\n\tactual         interface{}\n\tcontext        string\n\tresponseSnippet string\n\tfieldName      string\n\tpatternDetails string\n\trangeInfo      string\n\tvalidationDetails []string\n\tcustomSuggestions []string\n\tcategory       ErrorCategory\n}\n\n// NewValidationFormatter creates a new ValidationFormatter for the given validation type.\n//\n// This is the entry point for the builder pattern. The validationType identifies what\n// kind of validation was performed and is used to:\n//   - Categorize the error in the error message\n//   - Generate appropriate default suggestions\n//   - Provide context for error handling\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - validationType: The category of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n//\n//     Common validation types include:\n//     - \"status_code\": HTTP status code validation\n//     - \"error_message\": Error message pattern matching\n//     - \"content_type\": Content-Type header validation\n//     - \"status_code_range\": Status code range validation (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n//     - \"cors_headers\": CORS header validation\n//     - \"response_structure\": Response body structure validation\n//     - Custom types for domain-specific validation\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// A pointer to a new ValidationFormatter instance ready for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example Usage\n//\n//\t// Basic status code validation\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n//\t    WithExpected(200).\n//\t    WithActual(404).\n//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n//\t    Format()\n//\n//\t// Content-Type validation with context\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n//\t    WithExpected(\"application/json\").\n//\t    WithActual(\"text/html\").\n//\t    WithContext(\"API response\").\n//\t    Format()\n//\n//\t// Custom validation type with all options\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field_check\").\n//\t    WithExpected(\"non_empty\").\n//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n//\t    WithFieldName(\"username\").\n//\t    WithContext(\"User creation\").\n//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Username cannot be empty\", \"Must be 3-20 characters\").\n//\t    Format()\nfunc NewValidationFormatter(validationType string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\treturn &ValidationFormatter{\n\t\tvalidationType: validationType,\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithExpected sets the expected value for validation.\n//\n// This method specifies what value was expected during the validation check.\n// The expected value is compared against the actual value to provide clear\n// error messages showing what went wrong.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - expected: The expected value (can be any type: int, string, []int, etc.)\n//\n//     Common types:\n//     - int: Single expected value (e.g., expected status code 200)\n//     - []int: Multiple acceptable values (e.g., []int{200, 201, 204})\n//     - string: Expected pattern or literal value (e.g., \"application/json\")\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Single expected value\n//\tformatter.WithExpected(200)\n//\n//\t// Multiple acceptable values\n//\tformatter.WithExpected([]int{200, 201, 204})\n//\n//\t// String pattern\n//\tformatter.WithExpected(\"application/json\")\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithExpected(expected interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.expected = expected\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithActual sets the actual value received during validation.\n//\n// This method specifies what value was actually received during the validation check.\n// The actual value is compared against the expected value to show the discrepancy\n// that caused the validation failure.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - actual: The actual value received (can be any type: int, string, etc.)\n//\n//     Common types:\n//     - int: Actual numeric value (e.g., actual status code 404)\n//     - string: Actual message or content received\n//     - Other types as appropriate for the validation context\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Status code that was received\n//\tformatter.WithActual(404)\n//\n//\t// Error message that was received\n//\tformatter.WithActual(\"access denied\")\n//\n//\t// Content-Type that was received\n//\tformatter.WithActual(\"text/html\")\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithActual(actual interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.actual = actual\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithContext sets additional context about the validation operation.\n//\n// Context provides valuable information about where or when the validation occurred,\n// making errors easier to debug and understand. This is especially useful in logs\n// and test output where multiple validations might be happening.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - context: A descriptive string explaining the validation context\n//\n//     Common context examples:\n//     - HTTP endpoints: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n//     - Operation descriptions: \"OAuth token validation\", \"User creation\"\n//     - Test scenarios: \"Positive case test\", \"Negative case test\"\n//     - Component names: \"Authentication service\", \"Database query\"\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// HTTP endpoint context\n//\tformatter.WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\")\n//\n//\t// Operation context\n//\tformatter.WithContext(\"OAuth token validation\")\n//\n//\t// Test scenario context\n//\tformatter.WithContext(\"Testing unauthorized access\")\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithContext(context string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.context = context\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithResponseSnippet sets a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n//\n// Response snippets provide crucial debugging information by showing the actual\n// content that caused the validation failure. This is particularly useful when\n// debugging complex response structures or unexpected error messages.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - snippet: A truncated excerpt from the response (recommended: 100-200 characters)\n//\n//     Best practices:\n//     - Keep snippets concise (100-200 characters recommended)\n//     - Include relevant parts that show the problem\n//     - Truncate long responses with \"...\" to keep errors readable\n//     - Sanitize sensitive data before including in snippets\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Short JSON response snippet\n//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"error\": \"invalid_token\", \"code\": \"AUTH_001\"}`)\n//\n//\t// Truncated HTML snippet\n//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(`<html><body>Error: Page not found...</body></html>`)\n//\n//\t// Error message snippet\n//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(\"Access denied: insufficient permissions for resource\")\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.responseSnippet = snippet\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithFieldName sets the specific field name where the error was found.\n//\n// This method identifies the exact field that failed validation, which is especially\n// useful when validating structured data like JSON responses, form fields, or\n// configuration objects.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - fieldName: The name of the field where the error occurred\n//\n//     Common examples:\n//     - Response body fields: \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"code\"\n//     - Form fields: \"email\", \"password\", \"username\"\n//     - Nested fields: \"user.email\", \"data.items[0].id\"\n//     - Headers: \"Content-Type\", \"Authorization\"\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Simple field name\n//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"error\")\n//\n//\t// Nested field notation\n//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"user.email\")\n//\n//\t// Array field notation\n//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"items[0].id\")\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithFieldName(fieldName string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.fieldName = fieldName\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithPatternDetails sets information about pattern matching failures.\n//\n// This method provides additional context when validation involves pattern matching,\n// such as regex validation for error messages, format checking, or content verification.\n// It helps explain what pattern was expected and how the actual value didn't match.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - details: Information about the pattern that failed to match\n//\n//     Common examples:\n//     - Regex patterns: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n//     - Format requirements: \"expected format 'user@domain.com'\"\n//     - Pattern descriptions: \"must contain at least one uppercase letter\"\n//     - Match details: \"pattern matched at position 5 but failed validation\"\n//\n// # When to Use\n//\n// Use this method when:\n//   - Validating error messages against regex patterns\n//   - Checking string formats (email, phone, UUID, etc.)\n//   - Verifying content patterns in responses\n//   - Any validation involving pattern matching\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Regex pattern explanation\n//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\")\n//\n//\t// Format requirement\n//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"expected format: YYYY-MM-DD\")\n//\n//\t// Content pattern description\n//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"must contain at least 8 characters\")\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithPatternDetails(details string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.patternDetails = details\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithRangeInfo sets range boundaries for range validation failures.\n//\n// This method provides context when validating against ranges, such as HTTP status\n// code ranges (4xx, 5xx), numeric ranges, or value intervals. It helps explain the\n// expected boundaries and what range the actual value fell outside of.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - info: Description of the range boundaries\n//\n//     Common examples:\n//     - Status code ranges: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n//     - Numeric ranges: \"0-100\", \"1-1000\"\n//     - Descriptive ranges: \"positive numbers only\", \"non-negative integers\"\n//\n// # When to Use\n//\n// Use this method when:\n//   - Validating HTTP status code ranges (e.g., expecting 4xx, got 2xx)\n//   - Checking numeric value ranges (e.g., age 0-120)\n//   - Verifying values within intervals (e.g., percentage 0-100)\n//   - Any validation involving range constraints\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Status code range\n//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"400-499 (Client Error)\")\n//\n//\t// Numeric range\n//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"0-120\")\n//\n//\t// Descriptive range\n//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"positive integers only\")\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithRangeInfo(info string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.rangeInfo = info\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithValidationDetails adds additional validation-specific details.\n//\n// This method allows you to provide granular, multi-line information about what was\n// checked during validation and specifically what failed. This is useful for complex\n// validations that check multiple conditions or have detailed requirements.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - details: Variable number of detail strings to add to the error\n//\n//     Common uses:\n//     - List specific conditions that failed\n//     - Explain validation rules in detail\n//     - Provide step-by-step validation results\n//     - Document intermediate validation states\n//\n// # When to Use\n//\n// Use this method when:\n//   - Validation involves multiple checks or conditions\n//   - You need to explain complex validation rules\n//   - Detailed debugging information would be helpful\n//   - The validation logic is non-trivial\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Multiple specific failures\n//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n//\t    \"Password must be at least 8 characters\",\n//\t    \"Password must contain uppercase letters\",\n//\t    \"Password must contain special characters\",\n//\t)\n//\n//\t// Detailed explanation\n//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n//\t    \"Expected 200-299 status range\",\n//\t    \"Got 500 indicating server error\",\n//\t    \"Server logs show database connection failure\",\n//\t)\n//\n//\t// Step-by-step validation\n//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n//\t    \"Step 1: Check format - PASSED\",\n//\t    \"Step 2: Check length - FAILED\",\n//\t    \"Step 3: Check content - SKIPPED\",\n//\t)\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.validationDetails = append(vf.validationDetails, details...)\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithSuggestions sets custom suggestions for fixing the validation failure.\n//\n// This method allows you to provide domain-specific, actionable recommendations for\n// resolving the validation error. Custom suggestions override the auto-generated\n// suggestions that would normally be created based on the validation type and values.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - suggestions: Variable number of suggestion strings\n//\n//     Best practices for suggestions:\n//     - Be specific and actionable (not just \"check the value\")\n//     - Provide concrete steps to fix the issue\n//     - Include debugging tips when relevant\n//     - Reference documentation or resources when applicable\n//     - Keep suggestions concise but informative\n//     - Order from most to least likely solution\n//\n// # When to Use Custom Suggestions\n//\n// Use this method when:\n//   - Domain-specific knowledge is needed (not generic suggestions)\n//   - You want to guide users to specific solutions\n//   - Auto-generated suggestions would be too generic\n//   - You need to reference internal documentation or systems\n//   - The fix requires specific domain expertise\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Domain-specific suggestions\n//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n//\t    \"Check if the OAuth token has expired\",\n//\t    \"Verify the token has 'read:users' scope\",\n//\t    \"Contact admin to refresh permissions\",\n//\t)\n//\n//\t// Specific debugging steps\n//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n//\t    \"Check service logs at /var/log/api/errors.log\",\n//\t    \"Verify database connection in config.yaml\",\n//\t    \"Test with curl: curl -v http://localhost:8080/health\",\n//\t)\n//\n//\t// Reference documentation\n//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n//\t    \"See API docs: https://docs.example.com/errors#404\",\n//\t    \"Check troubleshooting guide: /docs/troubleshooting.md\",\n//\t)\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.customSuggestions = append(vf.customSuggestions, suggestions...)\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithCategory sets the category for the validation error.\n//\n// This method specifies the high-level categorization of the validation error.\n// If not set, the category will be auto-derived from the validation type.\n//\n// # Parameters\n//\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value (e.g., CategoryHTTP, CategoryContent, etc.)\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\tformatter.WithCategory(validate.CategoryHTTP)\n//\n//\t// With chaining\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n//\t    WithExpected(200).\n//\t    WithActual(404).\n//\t    WithCategory(validate.CategoryHTTP).\n//\t    Format()\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithCategory(category ErrorCategory) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.category = category\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// Format creates the final ValidationError with all configured options.\n//\n// This is the final method in the builder chain that constructs the ValidationError\n// with all the options that have been set. The returned ValidationError implements\n// the error interface and can be returned directly or used in error handling.\n//\n// # Behavior\n//\n// The Format() method:\n//   - Uses custom suggestions if provided via WithSuggestions\n//   - Auto-generates suggestions if no custom ones were set\n//   - Includes all fields that were set via With* methods\n//   - Returns a ValidationError ready for use\n//   - Implements error interface for immediate use\n//\n// # Auto-generated Suggestions\n//\n// If WithSuggestions was not called, suggestions are auto-generated based on:\n//   - validationType (e.g., status_code gets endpoint-checking suggestions)\n//   - expected and actual values (e.g., 404 gets resource-not-found suggestions)\n//   - Common patterns for the validation category\n//\n// # Returns\n//\n// A ValidationError struct populated with all configured fields that implements\n// the error interface and can be used immediately in error handling, logging, or\n// test assertions.\n//\n// # Example\n//\n//\t// Complete usage example\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n//\t    WithExpected(200).\n//\t    WithActual(404).\n//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n//\t    Format()\n//\n//\t// Error() method will produce:\n//\t// status_code validation failed\n//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n//\t//   Suggestions:\n//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n//\n//\t// Can be used directly as an error\n//\treturn err\n//\n//\t// Or formatted for logging\n//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n// formatErrorCore is the core error formatting function with enum-based category lookup.\n// This function implements the central category lookup logic using GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum\n// to map error types to their categories. It handles unknown error types by returning CategoryCustom\n// as the default category.\n//\n// This is a low-level function that provides type-safe error type to category mapping.\n// Most code should use ValidationFormatter.Format() or the Format*Error() convenience functions\n// instead of calling this directly.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n//   - message: The error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//   - expected: Optional expected value\n//   - actual: Optional actual value\n//   - context: Optional context information\n//\n// Returns a ValidationError with the category automatically derived from the error type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := formatErrorCore(\n//\t    ErrTypeRequired,\n//\t    \"Field is required\",\n//\t    \"email\",\n//\t    nil,\n//\t    \"\",\n//\t    \"user creation\",\n//\t)\n//\t// Returns ValidationError with Category = CategoryValidation\nfunc formatErrorCore(\n\terrorType ErrorType,\n\tmessage string,\n\tfieldName string,\n\texpected interface{},\n\tactual interface{},\n\tcontext string,\n) ValidationError {\n\t// Lookup category using the error type enum\n\t// GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum handles unknown error types by returning CategoryCustom\n\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\n\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for ValidationError\n\terrorTypeStr := string(errorType)\n\n\t// Create and return ValidationError with populated category\n\treturn ValidationError{\n\t\tErrorType: errorTypeStr,\n\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\tCategory:  category,\n\t\tExpected:  expected,\n\t\tActual:    actual,\n\t\tContext:   context,\n\t\tFieldName: fieldName,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) Format() ValidationError {\n\tsuggestions := vf.customSuggestions\n\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n\t\t// Use auto-generated suggestions if no custom ones provided\n\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate a concise message from validation parts\n\tmessage := generateMessageFromParts(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n\n\t// Determine category - use explicitly set category or auto-derive from error type\n\tcategory := vf.category\n\tif category == \"\" {\n\t\tcategory = GetCategoryForErrorType(vf.validationType)\n\t}\n\n\t// Construct ValidationError directly to support custom suggestions\n\treturn ValidationError{\n\t\tErrorType:         vf.validationType,\n\t\tMessage:           message,\n\t\tCategory:          category,\n\t\tExpected:          vf.expected,\n\t\tActual:            vf.actual,\n\t\tContext:           vf.context,\n\t\tResponseSnippet:   vf.responseSnippet,\n\t\tFieldName:         vf.fieldName,\n\t\tPatternDetails:    vf.patternDetails,\n\t\tRangeInfo:         vf.rangeInfo,\n\t\tValidationDetails: vf.validationDetails,\n\t\tSuggestions:       suggestions,\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// EXTENDING THE FORMATTER FOR NEW VALIDATION TYPES\n// =============================================================================\n//\n// This section explains how to extend the ValidationFormatter to support new\n// validation types beyond the built-in convenience functions.\n//\n// # Creating Custom Validation Types\n//\n// To add support for a new validation type, you have several options:\n//\n// ## Option 1: Use the Builder Pattern Directly\n//\n// The simplest approach is to use NewValidationFormatter with your custom type:\n//\n//\t// Custom header validation\n//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_header\").\n//\t    WithExpected(\"X-Custom-Header: required\").\n//\t    WithActual(\"missing\").\n//\t    WithContext(\"API request validation\").\n//\t    WithFieldName(\"X-Custom-Header\").\n//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Add X-Custom-Header to requests\", \"See API docs\").\n//\t    Format()\n//\n// ## Option 2: Create a Convenience Function\n//\n// For commonly used validation types, create a convenience function following\n// the pattern of existing functions like FormatStatusCodeError:\n//\n//\t// FormatCustomHeaderError validates custom HTTP headers\n//\tfunc FormatCustomHeaderError(headerName, expected, actual, context string) validate.ValidationError {\n//\t    return validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_header\").\n//\t        WithExpected(expected).\n//\t        WithActual(actual).\n//\t        WithFieldName(headerName).\n//\t        WithContext(context).\n//\t        WithSuggestions(\n//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Add %s header to request\", headerName),\n//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected value: %s\", expected),\n//\t        ).\n//\t        Format()\n//\t}\n//\n// ## Option 3: Use FormatCustomValidationError\n//\n// For advanced scenarios requiring full control, use FormatCustomValidationError:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n//\t    \"response_time\",\n//\t    \"< 100ms\",\n//\t    \"250ms\",\n//\t    validate.WithContext(\"API performance check\"),\n//\t    validate.WithValidationDetails(\"SLA requires < 100ms response time\"),\n//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Optimize database queries\", \"Add caching\"),\n//\t)\n//\n// # Best Practices for New Validation Types\n//\n// When creating custom validation types:\n//\n// 1. **Use descriptive type names**: Choose clear, descriptive validation type names\n//    that indicate what was validated (e.g., \"response_time\", \"header_presence\").\n//\n// 2. **Provide specific suggestions**: Custom suggestions should be actionable and\n//    specific to your domain rather than generic.\n//\n// 3. **Include relevant context**: Always provide context about where/when the\n//    validation occurred to aid debugging.\n//\n// 4. **Use appropriate field types**: Match the expected/actual field types to what\n//    you're validating (int for codes, string for patterns, etc.).\n//\n// 5. **Consider validation details**: Use WithValidationDetails for complex\n//    validations with multiple steps or conditions.\n//\n// 6. **Document your validation type**: If creating a convenience function, document\n//    it thoroughly like the existing convenience functions.\n//\n// # Integration with Auto-generated Suggestions\n//\n// The ValidationFormatter automatically generates suggestions based on validation\n// type when you don't provide custom suggestions. To integrate your custom type\n// with this system, you can modify the generateSuggestions function to recognize\n// your new type and provide domain-appropriate suggestions.\n//\n// Current auto-generated suggestion patterns:\n//   - \"status_code\": Endpoint and resource verification suggestions\n//   - \"error_message\": Error analysis and debugging suggestions\n//   - \"content_type\": Header and format verification suggestions\n//   - \"status_code_range\": Range-specific debugging suggestions\n//\n// # Example: Adding a New Validation Type\n//\n// Here's a complete example of adding a \"response_time\" validation type:\n//\n//\t// 1. Create the convenience function\n//\tfunc FormatResponseTimeError(expectedMaxMs, actualMs int, endpoint string) validate.ValidationError {\n//\t    return validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"response_time\").\n//\t        WithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"< %dms\", expectedMaxMs)).\n//\t        WithActual(fmt.Sprintf(\"%dms\", actualMs)).\n//\t        WithContext(endpoint).\n//\t        WithValidationDetails(\n//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"SLA requirement: < %dms\", expectedMaxMs),\n//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Actual response time: %dms\", actualMs),\n//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"SLA violation: %dms over limit\", actualMs-expectedMaxMs),\n//\t        ).\n//\t        WithSuggestions(\n//\t            \"Check for slow database queries\",\n//\t            \"Verify network latency\",\n//\t            \"Review application performance metrics\",\n//\t            \"Consider implementing caching\",\n//\t        ).\n//\t        Format()\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// 2. Use it in your code\n//\terr := FormatResponseTimeError(100, 250, \"GET /api/users\")\n//\tlog.Printf(\"Performance validation failed: %v\", err)\n//\n// This approach ensures your custom validation types integrate seamlessly with\n// the existing error formatting system while providing domain-specific guidance.\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON VALIDATION SCENARIOS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - expected: The expected status code (e.g., 200) or codes (e.g., []int{200, 201})\n//   - actual: The actual status code received\n//   - context: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"GET /api/users\")\n//\n// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n//\t// Output:\n//\t// status_code validation failed\n//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n//\t//   Suggestions:\n//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\nfunc FormatStatusCodeError(expected interface{}, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n\t\tWithActual(actual).\n\t\tWithContext(context).\n\t\tFormat()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorMessageError creates a validation error for error message pattern mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - expectedPattern: The pattern that was expected (e.g., \"invalid.*token\", \"not found\")\n//   - actualMessage: The actual error message received\n//   - fieldName: The field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\")\n//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n//\n// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatErrorMessageError(\"invalid.*token\", \"access_denied\", \"error\", \"OAuth validation\")\n//\t// Output:\n//\t// error_message validation failed\n//\t//   Expected: invalid.*token\n//\t//   Actual:   access_denied\n//\t//   Context:  OAuth validation\n//\t//   Field:    error\n//\t//   Suggestions:\n//\t//     - Review the error message for specific details\n//\t//     - Check API documentation for this error type\n//\t//     - Verify request parameters match requirements\nfunc FormatErrorMessageError(expectedPattern, actualMessage, fieldName, context string) ValidationError {\n\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n\t\tWithExpected(expectedPattern).\n\t\tWithActual(actualMessage).\n\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n\t\tWithContext(context).\n\t\tFormat()\n}\n\n// FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n//   - actual: The actual status code received\n//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name being validated (e.g., \"status_code\", \"response_code\")\n//\n// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\", \"status_code\")\n//\t// Output:\n//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n//\t//   Field:    status_code\n//\t//   Expected: 4xx (400-499)\n//\t//   Actual:   200\n//\t//   Context:  error response check\n//\t//   Suggestions:\n//\t//     - Review request parameters for errors\n//\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\nfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string, fieldName string) ValidationError {\n\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\n\n\t// Build range info string\n\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", min, max)\n\tif desc != \"\" {\n\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", min, max, desc)\n\t}\n\n\tdetails := []string{}\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern error: %v\", err))\n\t} else {\n\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside range %d-%d\", actual, min, max))\n\t}\n\n\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", pattern, desc)).\n\t\tWithActual(actual).\n\t\tWithContext(context).\n\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...)\n\n\t// Only add range info if the pattern is valid\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(rangeInfo)\n\t}\n\n\treturn formatter.Format()\n}\n\n// FormatContentTypeError creates a validation error for Content-Type header mismatches.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   -
